key_requirements: Module dependency graph: agents depend on llm, tools, security, stream, context, storage, integrations
Container diagram shows system interfaces with external APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, GitHub, GitLab, Jira)
Class hierarchy of core traits: Agent, LLMProvider, Tool, Storage, PlatformIntegration, StreamHandler, ApprovalHandler
Required for understanding what components to expose in PyO3 bindings
Core data types: Message, Role (system/user/assistant/tool), ToolCall, ToolResult, TokenUsage
All types are JSON Schema defined and will need Py* wrappers
Role enum, Message with tool_calls array, ToolResult with is_error flag
Critical for Python interoperability - these must be serializable to/from dicts
Tool trait: name(), description(), parameters(), execute(arguments), definition(), validate_arguments()
ToolRegistry class: register(), unregister(), get(), contains(), tool_names(), count()
ToolExecutor class: execute() with timeout and retry support
ToolParameter schema for parameter definition
Critical for register_python_tool() signature from pre-clarifications
Agent trait: run(input), run_with_handler(input, handler)
CodingAgent and AnalystAgent implementations (4 agents mentioned in pre-clarifications: RestructureIssueAgent, ReferenceContextAgent, ChangeSpecAgent, CodeAgent)
Agent execution loop sequence: CompletionRequest, tool calls, StreamHandler events, approval flow
Builder pattern: CodingAgentBuilder, AnalystAgentBuilder with fluent API
Default tools per agent type
Main entry points for Python API
LLMProvider trait: provider_name(), supported_models(), validate_model(), complete(), complete_stream()
Three implementations: ClaudeProvider, OpenAIProvider, GeminiProvider
CompletionRequest and CompletionResponse schemas
Supported models list per provider
Message conversion per provider (role mappings differ by provider)
Python needs to configure LLM providers
NovaError enum: 20+ variants (LLMError, ToolError, ToolNotFound, ApprovalDenied, SecurityViolation, ContextOverflow, etc.)
Error classification: retriable (HttpError, Timeout, StreamError, ApiError 429/5xx) vs non-retriable
User action required errors: ApprovalRequired, ApprovalDenied, SecurityViolation, PathNotAllowed, CommandNotAllowed, Config, ModelNotFound
Python exception hierarchy must map to this enum
Storage trait: save_session(), load_session(), list_sessions(), delete_session(), session_exists()
SessionState schema: id, title, status (active/paused/completed/cancelled), messages, notes, findings
MemoryStorage and FileStorage implementations
Session lifecycle state machine for AnalystAgent
Python needs to persist and resume agent sessions
StreamEvent schema: 13 event types (started, thinking, text_chunk, tool_call_requested, approval_requested, etc.)
StreamHandler trait: on_event(), should_cancel()
NoOpHandler, PrintHandler, CollectingHandler, CallbackHandler implementations
Event flow state machine showing valid transitions
Python may need streaming support for real-time agent feedback
ContextManager class: add_message(), add_user_message(), add_assistant_message(), add_tool_result()
Methods: get_messages(), available_tokens(), message_count(), compress(), stats()
ContextStats schema with message_count, estimated_tokens, max_tokens, available_tokens
Preset configs: default_128k, default_32k
Python needs to manage conversation context for agents
SecurityPolicy schema: blocked_paths, allowed_paths, blocked_commands, tools_requiring_approval
ApprovalRequest and ApprovalResponse schemas
Path validation logic: blocked_paths check → allowed_paths check
Tool approval flow with ApprovalHandler trait
Python needs to configure security policies for agent execution
PlatformIntegration trait: name(), get_issue(), list_issues(), get_comments(), post_comment(), into_tools()
Three implementations: GitHubIntegration, GitLabIntegration, JiraIntegration
Issue schema: id, title, state (open/closed/in_progress/resolved/wontfix), author, labels, assignees
IssueFilter for querying issues
Generated tools per platform (github_get_issue, gitlab_list_issues, jira_search_issues)
AnalystAgent can be configured with platform integrations
Six coding tools: BashTool, ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, EditFileTool, GlobTool, GrepTool
Each tool has Input/Output/Constraints schemas
BashTool default timeout: 120s, max_output_bytes: 100KB
ReadFileTool: offset (1-based line), limit (default 2000)
EditFileTool validation: requires replace_all=true for multiple matches
GlobTool and GrepTool: max_results defaults, file_type support
Python needs documentation of available tools
Six analysis tools: AskUserTool, TakeNoteTool, RecordFindingTool, WebSearchTool, WebFetchTool, PostCommentTool
AskUserTool: pause/resume flow for AnalystAgent
TakeNoteTool: Note schema with category (requirement/risk/question/observation)
RecordFindingTool: Finding schema with severity (info/low/medium/high/critical)
WebSearchTool and WebFetchTool for research
PostCommentTool: posts clarification questions to GitHub/GitLab/Jira issues
Python users should understand these tools
ReferenceContextAgent: one of the 4 agents from pre-clarifications
Extends SpecStore trait with read(path) method for full text retrieval
Generates reference context with spec scoring (high/medium/low relevance)
Internal quality review via ReviewAgent (CRR cycle)
Output schema: specs array with spec_id, spec_group, relevance, key_requirements; contradictions array
Python needs to be able to create and run ReferenceContextAgent
RestructureIssueAgent: one of the 4 agents from pre-clarifications
Spec incomplete but represents part of the agent ecosystem
Python should be able to instantiate this agent type
ReviewAgent: used internally by other agents for quality review (CRR cycle)
Spec incomplete but mentioned in pre-clarifications as implemented
Python may need to configure or customize review behavior
